steps.title: Setting the Stage
narrative: Welcome to my weather disocvery lab.  My goal with this project is to bring to life the stories and events memorable weather events like the May 12, 2022 derecho across eastern SD, western MN, and northwest IA. I wanted this to be a unique expereience where the students get to exeperience what it's like to do my job.  I created a LIVE Doppler radar simulator and you'll see this tool often in my program.
Students will get to use some real life tools in a teaching enviroment that will prompt good questions to think about.

steps.title: Forecasters Saw the Risk Early
narrative: This region was highlighted for severe weather before storms fully developed.
Tap Details on the Moderate Risk outlook to read the setup and why forecasters were concerned.

steps.title: Surface Setup and Model Temperatures
narrative: This card combines the surface map and fronts with subtle wind particles so students can see the larger setup.
The 3 PM HRRR temperature layer is also turned on here so you can probe the model temperatures directly on the map.

steps.title: Dew Point
narrative: By 4 PM, richer low-level moisture had spread north into the target area.
This card now focuses only on METAR dew points so the moisture field is easy to read and explain.

steps.title: Now the Storm Comes Alive
narrative: Storms are moving toward Sioux Falls quickly!

My program takes students right back to the moment the storm was happening with real warning and even lightning strikes, giving a sense of real-time urgency to the lab.  It even comes with my very own storm tracker tool.

steps.title: Storm Reports
narrative: Storm reports help connect the weather map to what was happening on the ground.
This final card should wire directly to the reports layer so the impact is easy to see.
